description_long: This object consists of two roughly cuboid stone blocks placed side by side, each with irregular, naturalistic surfaces and slightly rounded edges. The stones display a mix of light gray and beige tones, with subtle greenish patches suggesting some moss or lichen growth. Their surfaces are uneven, with visible pitting, creases, and minor chips, giving them a weathered, aged appearance. The blocks are solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings, and their size suggests they could serve as low barriers or boundary markers in an urban or landscaped setting. The texture is coarse and tactile, typical of quarried or minimally processed stone. There are no visible engravings, markings, or artificial modifications beyond their basic shape.
description: Two weathered, roughly cuboid stone blocks with irregular, coarse surfaces and subtle greenish patches, placed side by side.
description_view_0: Front view showing both stone blocks side by side, highlighting their irregular faces and subtle color variations.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth and uneven vertical surfaces of the blocks, with more pronounced texture and edge rounding.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with slightly different surface details and color distribution on the blocks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the depth and irregularity of the stone surfaces, with visible weathering.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible surface pitting, minor chips, and greenish moss or lichen growth on some edges indicate moderate weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: placed adjacent to similar blocks, likely forming a low barrier or edging
